changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "The Glint in the Demon's Eye"

TextENGNew: "...Yes, that's right. ...So the wake will be at six PM tomorrow evening, and the memorial service the day after, from noon to one.@k@vS20/00/kanryoC44002.The venue is Okinomiya Ceremony Hall..."
"...I see. If it's the funeral of Deputy Mayor Ikezawa's grandson, we can't very well not pay our respects.@k@vS20/17/442300027.Mion, go in my stead."
``Okay, got it. Should I wear mourning clothes?@k@vS20/26/440300115. How much should I bring for the funeral offering?``

"...You can go in your school uniform. Make sure every button is done up properly.@k@vS20/17/442300029.For the offering... fifty -- no, make it a hundred thousand yen.@k@vS20/17/442300030.We owe them much."
Mion let out a low whistle at the generous sum.
"...Ikezawa-san has been a solid, reliable man since his days as head of the Okinomiya office. ...Government types may say they're paying a visit, but they never set foot past the front door."
"But whenever I offered Ikezawa-san tea, he'd always come inside and listen properly to what I had to say. ...A man who truly heard people out to the end. A model others should follow."
The two guests -- who were supposed to be the visitors -- nodded and murmured in exaggerated agreement as Oryou reminisced.
Watching them desperately try not to offend Oryou was comical enough to draw the occasional quiet laugh from Mion.
``How young was Deputy Mayor Ikezawa's grandson when he died?``
``...Umm... 11 years old, apparently...``
Oryou lowered her eyes and shook her head slowly, lamenting the loss of such a young life.
changeReason: "lowered her eyes" more accurately captures 目を伏せる (casting eyes downward) compared to "closed her eyes" which describes a different physical action.

TextENGNew: "I believe he said he only had the one grandchild... How pitiful...@k@vS20/17/442300035.There is no greater sin for a parent than to outlive their own flesh and blood."
``Ahahaha. You're really nice to other people, Granny.@k@vS20/26/440300117. If Shion or I died, would you be this sad?``
``Don't be stupid. Saying something like that is bad luck!``
Having got the reaction she expected, Mion cackled.
The two guests, unsure if they were supposed to laugh along, instead forced awkward smiles.
``We'll take our leave for today, then... We'll be by tomorrow evening at five to pick you up.@k@vS20/00/kanryoC44005. Until then...@k@vS20/00/kanryoC44006. please excuse us...``
After bowing their heads repeatedly, the guests left by the front door.
Mion saw them off, waving her little hand goodbye.

``...They're gone. ...It must be rough for them to pay you a courtesy visit, Granny.@k@vS20/26/440300119. It would have been fine if they just broke the news over the phone.@k@vS20/26/440300120. Heh heh heh...``
Indeed, Oryou Sonozaki held the village of Hinamizawa -- its residents and its extended family -- firmly in her grasp.
Translated into votes, that amounted to several thousand.
It was little wonder the mayor would want to curry favor with her.
Unlike the amused Mion, however, Oryou was gazing up at the sky from the veranda with a melancholy expression.
changeReason: "melancholy expression" more accurately captures 寂しそうな (lonely/sad-looking) compared to "longingly" which implies yearning rather than sadness.

TextENGNew: ``What's the matter? Are you going senile?@k@vS20/26/440300122. Ahahahaha!``
"...No matter whose grandchild it was... I can't help feeling sorry for them..."
Saying that, she let out a deep sigh.
Normally she would scold Mion with a sharp retort for her cheekiness, so this subdued response caught Mion off guard...
"Mion. ...That business with... the Minister's grandson.@k@vS20/17/442300039.How long has it been since he was taken?"
``Hmm. ...Four days, maybe?``
Oryou let out another deep sigh.
"Enemy's grandchild or not... I feel sorry for the boy."
``....Really?``
"We've given them enough of a scare. ...It's time to bring this to an end."
Every trace of playfulness drained from Mion's face, replaced by an expression of cold composure.
changeReason: "cold composure" more accurately captures 冷え切った (thoroughly chilled/cold) compared to "stern," conveying a different emotional quality.

TextENGNew: Then, as if to gauge Oryou's true intent, she peered deep into the old woman's eyes.
...And Oryou gazed back into Mion's, conveying her will through her eyes alone.
``...I want some tea, could you call for some? Some of that chocolate too.``
Mion gave a small nod, then turned around.
``Shimiko-san, are you there? Grandma wants some tea, could you prepare some?``
From far away, a voice responded in the affirmative.
Mion, after confirming she'd been heard, picked up the phone and began dialing.
``....Ah, hello? This is Mion.@k@vS20/26/440300128. ...Yeah.@k@vS20/26/440300129. ...Is my father there?``
